articles.title: Breaking: US-Iran MoU signed remotely by both Trump and Pezeshkian to end the brutal Middle East war
articles.description: A historic diplomatic breakthrough appears to have taken effect as the United States and Iran reportedly signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending months of conflict and opening a new phase of negotiations. According to multiple reports, U.S. President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed the agreement remotely, bringing the framework into force ahead of a planned diplomatic meeting. The deal is expected to focus on ending hostilities, restoring regional stability, reopening key shipping routes and launching further talks on Iran’s nuclear program, sanctions relief and broader security issues.

articles.title: ‘Just J**k off’: Trump Coins Nasty Nickname For Georgia’s Jon Ossoff As Collins Senate Race Heats Up
articles.description: President Donald Trump has sparked fresh controversy after unveiling a new nickname for Democratic Senator Jon Ossoff on Truth Social, referring to him as “Os(j**k!)off” while throwing his support behind Republican Rep. Mike Collins in Georgia’s high-stakes Senate race. Trump also promised to hold major rallies in the state, escalating the political battle ahead of the election.The remarks come as Republicans look to unseat Ossoff in one of the most closely watched contests of the 2026 cycle. Watch the latest developments, reactions, and what Trump’s endorsement could mean for the Georgia showdown.

articles.title: BJP Slams Rahul Gandhi’s Kota Outreach Ahead Of NEET Re-Test, Alleges ‘Politics Over Students’
articles.description:
New Delhi, Jun 17 (PTI) Ahead of Rahul Gandhi's interaction with students in Kota, the BJP on Wednesday accused the Congress leader of playing politics at the expense of medical aspirants who are in the final leg of preparations for the June 21 NEET-UG re-exam.

Addressing a press conference at the BJP headquarters, party national spokesperson Sudhanshu Trivedi asked why Gandhi chose Kota in Rajasthan for his interaction with the students 72 hours before the NEET-UG retest and accused him of being "cunning" for political gains.

Trivedi said, "Is it because of the Congress' internal politics that you want to play with the students' sentiments there?" Gandhi will interact with students in Kota in the evening as part of the Congress' campaign against paper leaks and to highlight youth issues.

The programme, to be held at an open theatre in Kota, is being positioned by the Congress as the first in a series of nationwide student conventions.

The campaign seeks to highlight the challenges faced by young people due to alleged paper leaks, rising examination costs and what the party described as the government's failure to ensure a fair and transparent recruitment and education system.

Slamming Gandhi, Trivedi asked, "At a time when the students are focused on the last leg of their preparations for the NEET-UG exam, why do you want to distract them and subject them to mental torture?" "Give the students the opportunity to prepare for their exam during these 72 hours without any mental stress. Refrain from playing politics during this sensitive time and disrupting their state of mind and preparation by subjecting them to mental anguish," the BJP leader said.

"There will be many opportunities to play politics. Politics can happen after June 21," he said. PTI PK DIV

(This report has been published as part of the auto-generated syndicate wire feed. Apart from the headline, no editing has been done in the copy by ABP Live.)

articles.title: Telegram moves Delhi HC against Centre’s ban ahead of NEET-UG re-test; court to hear plea today
articles.description: Telegram has moved the Delhi High Court against the Centre's temporary ban. The government imposed the restriction ahead of the NEET-UG re-test scheduled for June 21. The court agreed to hear the petition today. This move aims to ensure the smooth conduct of the examination. The ban is a measure taken by the government.
